## [00:00 - 02:52]

This is a pubcast shot. Be amazed. I'm John Loomer. Let's go. I've talked before about how important the Facebook ads funnel strategy is to my approach. But something else to keep in mind is, you know, at the very top when I'm just driving traffic to my website, you'd be amazed at some of the unintended conversions you can get out of that. So an example, I have a campaign that's constantly running that's promoting blog posts. It's a long story how I get it set up. But it's also spending like 10 to 20 dollars a day. I can't remember what that budget is, but it's not a lot. It might be 20 dollars a day. And I'm only targeting a very high-quality audience and I'm optimizing actually for high-quality traffic. And that's a whole other discussion in terms of how I do that. But the bottom line is this. I'm only trying to get traffic out of this campaign. And over the course of this campaign now, I've spent almost $800 and it's generated 20 purchases. So we've got about a $40 cost per purchase on something that all I was trying to do was drive traffic to my website. So I think that there are two parts of that. First of all, my focus was on quality traffic. So I'm optimizing for that whole another topic. But the second is making sure your website is set up in a way that if someone likes what you are saying, they can easily buy something. Make sure you cross the link across your website to your products. Make sure you've got promotions that are happening in there. It could be, you know, little banner ads and stuff like that. Just make sure that if you hook them with your content, that it's easy for them to them buy. Because what's buried in there in your ad reports is purchases that you didn't even know happened.
